rationale: A visible escalation along the border will exploit enemy uncertainty and deter preemptive strikes while buying time for economic stabilization.
Deploying fleet assets near the Tethys maritime corridor pressures energy transit routes and signals resolve without immediate kinetic action.
A precision strike on a forward reconnaissance unit could disrupt Defender planning cycles and test their reaction time under crisis conditions.
Leverage the cyber breach to disable command nodes in Defender logistics networks, creating strategic delay and amplifying confusion.
Stage a minor sabotage event implicating a third-party actor to fracture Defender alliances and prompt internal suspicion.
Conduct an internal audit of our own vulnerabilities exposed during the breach to prevent retaliatory exploitation.
Secure reserves of critical energy inputs now to insulate the economy from potential Tethys pipeline disruptions or sanctions escalation.
Open quiet talks with neutral powers to diversify export routes and reduce dependency on Tethys-controlled infrastructure.
Subtly weaken the national currency to make Tethys’ import-dependent economy more vulnerable to inflationary pressures, eroding their domestic stability.
A high-visibility mobilization along the Tethys border demonstrates resolve without triggering direct escalation, leveraging our defensive positioning as a deterrent.
Disrupt Novaris’ command-and-control systems in border regions to degrade their operational readiness and signal asymmetric retaliation capability.
Position naval assets near critical pipeline infrastructure to implicitly threaten energy flow disruption if Novaris continues its mobilization.
Conduct a controlled false flag cyber operation to sow confusion within Novaris’ military leadership about the source of the breach, buying time for strategic repositioning.
Deploy deep-source HUMINT networks to assess Novaris’ decision-making thresholds and identify potential fracture points in their alliance structure.
Exfiltrate data on Novaris’ energy sector dependencies to prepare for future economic leverage or blackmail in negotiations.
Use neutral intermediaries to open quiet talks with key regional powers, framing Novaris’ actions as a systemic threat to regional stability.
Launch a domestic and regional humanitarian narrative showcasing Tethys as a responsible actor under pressure, countering potential Novaris-led disinformation.
Propose third-party mediation through international institutions to de-escalate tensions, positioning Tethys as the peace-seeking party and isolating Novaris diplomatically.
Accelerate reserves accumulation of critical energy and food supplies to extend endurance beyond 11 weeks, reducing vulnerability to blockade or export cuts.
Secure emergency supply deals with non-aligned states to diversify import channels and reduce dependence on Novaris-controlled logistics routes.
Slightly devalue the national currency to boost export competitiveness and generate short-term revenue to fund crisis measures without triggering inflationary collapse.
